text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.uiBrightn:
In nits, the paper white of UI elements and vanilla game (i.e without shaders).
Set it negative to use display reported SDR paper white.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.customGam:
In nits, the paper white of the game/scene.
Set it negative to use display reported value.

In nits, the minimum the display can output.
(Linux / Window with DX-GL interop) Set it negative to use display reported value. Leave 0 if unsure.

In nits, the maximum the display can output.
(Linux / Window with DX-GL interop) Set it negative to use display reported value.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.customEot:
Correct raised shadows to match SDR.
Use if OS or display doesn't do so already.
 Set it negative to use display reported SDR paper white.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.screensho:
Set to higher than 1 to downscale the image with nearest neighbour method.
 Values lower than 1 have the same effect as 1.
 Useful when used with mods like RenderScale.

Higher level saves disk space but takes more time,
Lower level takes more disk space but saves time.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.behaviorO: Behavior When Other Mods Call Vanilla Screenshot Method

ONLY_VANILLA means only taking vanilla screenshot,
BOTH means taking both vanilla and HDR screenshot, 
ONLY_HDR means only taking HDR screenshot.
Behavior When Taking Vanilla F2 Screenshot

ONLY_VANILLA means only taking vanilla screenshot,
BOTH means taking both vanilla and HDR screenshot, 
ONLY_HDR means only taking HDR screenshot.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableRep: Enable Replay Mod HDR Video Export
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.replayRec: Replay Mod HDR Video Export Recommended Command Line
Changing the command line here have NO ACTUAL EFFECT, it's just convenient for copying.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.replayUIB: UI Brightness When Replay Mod Rendering
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.replayGam: Game Paper White Brightness When Replay Mod Rendering
Game Minimum Brightness When Replay Mod Rendering
Game Peak Brightness When Replay Mod Rendering
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableCha: Enable Vanilla GLFW Character Callback Replacement
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableIMB: Enable IMBlocker Set Input Method State Integration
Enable IMBlocker Set Preedit Overlay Position Integration
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.PreeditOv: Divide By Monitor Scale When Setting Preedit Overlay Position
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableIMB: Enable IMBlocker Set Input Method English State Integration
Enable IMBlocker Set Preedit Callback Integration
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableCha: Necessary for input methods proper working on some platforms.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableIMB: Use native Wayland protocols in modified GLFW instead of commandline to control IME on/off.
Use LWJGL 3.4.1 function to set IME overlay position.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.PreeditOv: Necessary for some platforms to make IME overlay position correct.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.enableIMB: Use IMBlocker original method to control IME English/CJK state.
Use LWJGL 3.4.1 callback to get IME preedit screen so IMBlocker can show preedit overlay on non-Windows platform.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.useUNORMW:
Switch between RGB10A2+BT.2100 PQ (better performance) or RGBA16F+scRGB (better precision) on Windows with GL-DX interop or Linux. Breaks HDR on Windows without GL-DX Interop.
Keep default if unsure.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.option.forceActi: Use OpenGL to render offscreen and use DirectX to present the result in HDR format. May cause huge performance loss and mod incompatibilities.
text.autoconfig.hdr_mod.title: HDR Mod Config
